The task is to find the minimum number of steps to make the password strong. Each step can be inserting, deleting, or replacing a character.

int missing = 0;
if (no lowercase) missing++;
if (no uppercase) missing++;

Then, calculate the repeating steps:

List<Integer> repeats = new ArrayList<>();
int i = 0;
while (i < password.length()) {
    int j = i;
    while (j < password.length() && password.charAt(j) == password.charAt(i)) {
        j++;
    }
    int len = j - i;
    if (len >=3) {
        repeats.add(len);
    }
    i = j;
}

Then, for each len in repeats, calculate the steps as len /3.

int repeatSteps = 0;
for (int r : repeats) {
    repeatSteps += r /3;
}

Now, handle the three cases.

int total = Math.max(add, missing + repeatSteps);

So, the code outline is:

public int strongPasswordChecker(String password) {
    int missing = 0;
    boolean hasLower = false, hasUpper = false, hasDigit = false;
    for (char c : password.toCharArray()) {
        if (Character.isLowerCase(c)) hasLower = true;
        else if (Character.isUpperCase(c)) hasUpper = true;
        else if (Character.isDigit(c)) hasDigit = true;
    }
    if (!hasLower) missing++;
    if (!hasUpper) missing++;
    if (!hasDigit) missing++;

    List<Integer> repeats = new ArrayList<>();
    int i = 0;
    while (i < password.length()) {
        int j = i;
        while (j < password.length() && password.charAt(j) == password.charAt(i)) j++;
        int len = j - i;
        if (len >=3) repeats.add(len);
        i = j;
    }

    int repeatSteps = 0;
    for (int r : repeats) {
        repeatSteps += r /3;
    }

    int len = password.length();
    int steps = 0;

    if (len <6) {
        int add = 6 - len;
        steps = Math.max(add, missing + repeatSteps);
    } else if (len >20) {
        int del = len -20;
        // Use deletions to reduce repeatSteps
        int usedDel = Math.min(del, repeatSteps);
        int remainingRepeats = repeatSteps - usedDel;
        steps = del + Math.max(missing, remainingRepeats);
    } else {
        steps = Math.max(missing, repeatSteps);
    }

    return steps;
}

So, the code outline is:

public int strongPasswordChecker(String password) {
    int missing = 0;
    boolean hasLower = false, hasUpper = false, hasDigit = false;
    for (char c : password.toCharArray()) {
        if (Character.isLowerCase(c)) hasLower = true;
        else if (Character.isUpperCase(c)) hasUpper = true;
        else if (Character.isDigit(c)) hasDigit = true;
    }
    if (!hasLower) missing++;
    if (!hasUpper) missing++;
    if (!hasDigit) missing++;

    List<Integer> repeats = new ArrayList<>();
    int i = 0;
    while (i < password.length()) {
        int j = i;
        while (j < password.length() && password.charAt(j) == password.charAt(i)) j++;
        int len = j - i;
        if (len >=3) repeats.add(len);
        i = j;
    }

    int repeatSteps = 0;
    for (int r : repeats) {
        repeatSteps += r /3;
    }

    int len = password.length();
    int steps = 0;

    if (len <6) {
        int add = 6 - len;
        steps = Math.max(add, missing + repeatSteps);
    } else if (len >20) {
        int del = len -20;
        // Use deletions to reduce repeatSteps
        int usedDel = Math.min(del, repeatSteps);
        int remainingRepeats = repeatSteps - usedDel;
        steps = del + Math.max(missing, remainingRepeats);
    } else {
        steps = Math.max(missing, repeatSteps);
    }

    return steps;
}
